content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886
December 2, 2002


David Bauer
400 Capitol Mall, Suite 1560
Sacramento, CA 95814

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. W-02-316

Dear Mr. Bauer:

This letter is in response to your request for written advice on the campaign provisions of the Political Reform Act.¹ Under California Code of Regulations 18329(b)(8)(B), copy enclosed, unless you are an authorized representative of the candidate for whom you seek assistance, the Fair Political Practices Commission cannot address your questions or provide analysis. Please review the enclosed pamphlet, "Getting Advice From the FPPC," prior to submission of future advice requests.

Should you have additional questions regarding this matter, please do not hesitate to contact me at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel

John W. Wallace
Assistant General Counsel

Enclosures
JWW/jg

_________________________

1. Government Code sections 81000-91014. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections 18109-18997, of the California Code of Regulations.

sections.question_synthetic: Can the FPPC provide advice on campaign provisions of the Political Reform Act to a person who is not an authorized representative of a candidate?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC cannot address questions or provide analysis unless the requester is an authorized representative of the candidate for whom advice is being sought, per California Code of Regulations 18329OX8)(B).
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is a declination letter. No formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections present. The letter references enclosed regulations and a pamphlet. Requestor appears to lack standing to receive FPPC advice under the applicable regulatory requirement.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Can the FPPC provide advice on campaign provisions of the Political Reform Act to a person who is not an authorized representative of a candidate?

CONCLUSION: The FPPC cannot address questions or provide analysis unless the requester is an authorized representative of the candidate for whom advice is being sought, per California Code of Regulations 18329OX8)(B).

embedding.summary: The FPPC declines to provide advice to David Bauer on campaign provisions of the Political Reform Act because he does not appear to be an authorized representative of a candidate, as required by regulations.
